title: Generate Installed Lifecycle manifest and baseline facts
outOfScope: Update, rollback, disable/enable, uninstall, purge, release publication, and final plan cleanup behavior
Project-owned files in an adopter repository
rawUserRequest: 请开始循环执行上面的计划，一直到全部工单完成为止，中途不要停。

declaredIntent.summary: Implement the first Installed Lifecycle Work Item: durable installation facts for later governed update, rollback, and uninstall operations.

sources.reason: Work Item 1 requires four durable installation fact files and fail-closed validation.
Existing installer is the installation transaction boundary.
Existing lifecycle fact source is the read-only status integration point.
Existing installer tests cover transactional install behavior.
Existing lifecycle fact tests cover generated state integration.
The measured implementation and regression tests require a bounded Python budget increase with repayment evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Fresh install writes manifest, version, managed-regions, and rollback-baseline facts
Repeated read produces stable hashes and ownership facts
Missing, malformed, or tampered facts fail closed
scenarioCoverage.reason: Later destructive operations must not guess from paths.

riskAssessment.reason: The new fact schema must remain compatible with future Work Items; all later operations are explicitly out of scope here.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized serial execution of all plan Work Items and this Work Item has bounded scope and evidence.

preReviewWarnings: Validate deterministic hashes, ownership vocabulary, and fail-closed behavior before any future destructive lifecycle Work Item.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Fresh installation creates .ai/install/manifest.json, version.json, managed-regions.json, and rollback-baseline.json with installationId, source/version, timestamps, ownership, source paths, and installed digests.

Manifest hash is recorded in version.json and all fact files are schema-validated, deterministically serialized, and read back with stable hashes; the measured Python budget change has an auditable repayment record.
Missing, malformed, or digest-tampered fact files cause a fail-closed validation result without guessing ownership or modifying project-owned content.
Installer and lifecycle documentation explain the fact files and tests cover fresh, repeated, malformed, and tampered cases.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The installer currently records only distribution version metadata; later lifecycle operations would have to guess ownership, managed regions, and rollback state from paths.
constraints: Keep the first Work Item limited to durable fact generation and validation; do not implement update or removal behavior yet.
Preserve project-owned and historical content and use deterministic SHA-256 facts.
Do not claim external provider, adopter, or release execution evidence.
intent.rationale: A durable, validated fact source is the prerequisite for safe three-way update, rollback, disable, and evidence-preserving uninstall operations.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized implementation, tests, docs, PRs, merges, lifecycle closure, and branch cleanup for the serial plan.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
